target.name: Dataminr
target.founder: Ted Bailey, Sam Hendel, Jeff Kinsey

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series F → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.3x) to $4.1B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.1B - $250.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Applied Intuition
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series F), post-money $15B

data.narrative:
Dataminr, a Series F AI & Software company, is being valued against a peer set of 8 private and 1 public comparable (Palantir). The target's last post-money valuation stands at $4.1B on $485M total raised. Key private peers include Applied Intuition ($15.0B, Series F), xAI ($250.0B, Series E), and SandboxAQ ($5.6B, Series E), with valuations ranging from $1.1B to $250.0B.

Two triangulation methods apply. The Stage-Progression method applies a 1.3x step-up multiplier typical for Series F advancement to the $4.1B base, implying $5.3B. The Peer-Median method yields $4.7B across the 8-company same-sector and same/adjacent-stage cohort. These methods produce a triangulated range of $4.0B (low) to $6.1B (high), with a median of $5.0B.

The analysis supports a valuation band of $4.0B–$6.1B, centered near $5.0B. The current $4.1B post-money sits at the lower end of this range. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion within the peer set—driven principally by xAI's outlier $250.0B valuation—and stage-progression rate variability in frontier AI. Removal of extreme outliers would tighten the range considerably but would require explicit justification beyond sector and stage alignment.
